_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Benign cystic ovarian masses (e.g., functional cysts, mature cystic teratomas, endometriomas, serous and mucinous cystadenomas, para-ovarian cysts)
clinical_indications: Benign cystic ovarian masses in premenopausal women
Functional cysts (follicular, haemorrhagic, and corpus luteum cysts)
Benign ovarian tumour more than 7 cm

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission
Other Tumor markers based on etiology (optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Did physical examination, imaging and investigation confirm the diagnosis?
Was there an evidence of cysts/tumor on Ultrasound report?

cpd_claim.q: Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Was malignancy ruled out in suspicious lesions?

common_queries: Physical examination, imaging, or investigation did not support the diagnosis or indication for surgery.
Ovarian tumor size not documented, or documented <= 7 cm without clear justification for surgical removal.
Malignancy not explicitly ruled out for suspicious lesions at claim stage.
Mandatory documents for pre-authorization (e.g., Detailed Clinical notes, USG Abdomen/pelvis, CA 125 Tumor marker, Planned line of treatment) are missing or incomplete.
Mandatory documents for claim submission (e.g., Detailed indoor case papers, Detailed procedure/operative notes, Histopathological Examination, Detailed Discharge Summary) are missing or incomplete.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Care at tertiary hospital; laparoscopic facility for laparoscopic procedures.
clinical_key_pointers: Laparoscopic ovarian cystectomy has now evolved to become the gold standard of surgical management of benign cystic ovarian masses in premenopausal women.
The optimal surgical goal is to remove the entire cyst intact. The cyst should be removed inside a laparoscopic bag so that inadvertent spillage into the peritoneal cavity may be avoided.

Ovarian conservation is generally the goal in a premenopausal woman with a benign ovarian cyst requiring surgical excision. The advantage is preservation of viable ovarian tissue and thus fertility and hormone production.
Ovarian cysts can occur at any stage in life from foetal life through menopause. They can be symptomatic or asymptomatic and found incidentally on clinical exam or on imaging.
It is important to differentiate between benign and malignant enlargements of the ovary to institute timely and effective treatment without undue delay.
surgical_options: Laparoscopic dissection of the cyst (in a young woman)
Hysterectomy with bilateral removal of adnexa (in an older woman)
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: The initial workup should include a medical history, physical exam, serial beta-HCG, Complete Blood Count, and ultrasound imaging. Depending on the presentation, physicians may also elect to check serial hematocrits and cervical cultures if a haemorrhagic cyst or abscess is suspected.
contraindications: The presence of a known malignancy has traditionally been considered an absolute contraindication.
